title: feat(vault): Lot 5 — watch daemon + CLI
summary: Implements Lot 5: vault watch daemon for incremental polling of vault changes with background daemon spawning, PID tracking, and synchronous watch cycles.

files_changed.purpose: Core watcher module with watchOnce() for single cycles, watchStatus() for daemon status, and watchLoop() for background polling
key_functions: watchOnce(vaultPath, updateFn, graphFn) — Synchronous cycle with SHA256 manifest hashing
watchStatus(vaultPath) — Check daemon status via PID
watchLoop(vaultPath, intervalSec, updateFn, graphFn) — Background polling with signal handling

files_changed.purpose: Daemon launcher script spawned detached from bin/vault.js
files_changed.key_logic: Parses --vault-path and --interval args, calls watchLoop() with polling interval clamped to min 5s

files_changed.purpose: Added watch command dispatch and helper functions
changes: Added imports: spawn from child_process, watchOnce/watchStatus from src/vault/watch.js
Added startVaultWatch(cwd, intervalSec) — spawns daemon detached with stdio:ignore
Added stopVaultWatch(cwd) — kills PID via SIGTERM, removes watch.json
Added onceVaultWatch(cwd) — synchronous cycle with update/graph callbacks
Added printWatch* functions for formatted output
Added 'watch' dispatch in runVault with actions: once|start|stop|status

tests: vault watch status sans daemon retourne { active: false }
vault watch start creates watch.json with pid

architecture.polling_strategy: File hashing via SHA256 of all vault/*.md files, stored in .peter/state.json lastWatchManifest
architecture.daemon_lifecycle: spawn detached + stdio:ignore, PID file in .peter/watch.json, SIGTERM cleanup
architecture.intervals: Configurable via --interval, clamped to min 5s, max ~30s default
architecture.event_logging: Appends to .peter/events.jsonl with watch_cycle events including elapsed, changedFiles, updateOk, graphOk

test_coverage.gate_status: PASSED — secrets, lint, build, tests all clean

review_focus: Daemon spawning logic — verify detached and stdio handling is correct
Signal handlers — SIGTERM/SIGINT cleanup in watchLoop
File hashing — SHA256 manifest comparison for change detection
Test isolation — ensure daemon processes don't leak between tests
integration_points: Calls updateVault() and graphVault() callbacks when changes detected
Uses existing vault/.peter/ directory for state and config storage
Compatible with vault update/graph commands for incremental rebuilds
next_steps_if_approved: Bump version in package.json and bin/cli.js
Verify npm publish with new version
Consider git hook installation if requested in Phase D
